* decision(OQ-1): fanout exchange for dual-indexer A/B test

Resolve the competing consumers problem blocking P1-3 and P1-4.

Decision: Use a RabbitMQ fanout exchange ('documents') so every
document message is delivered to both indexer queues independently.
Rejected publish-twice (tight coupling, partial failure risk) and
sequential indexing (doubles latency, complexity explosion).

Includes full implementation plan with exact file changes, env var
configs, migration order, and rollback procedure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(infra): add Docker Compose A/B setup for dual-indexer testing (#870)

Add embeddings-server-e5 and document-indexer-e5 services for A/B testing
distiluse (baseline) vs multilingual-e5-base (candidate) embeddings.

- embeddings-server-e5: e5-base model, 3GB memory limit, dev port 8086
- document-indexer-e5: reads shortembeddings_e5base queue, writes books_e5base
  collection, 300-char chunks with 50-char overlap
- Dockerfile: MODEL_NAME now a build ARG for multi-model image builds
- Both indexers depend on solr-init (service_completed_successfully)
- RabbitMQ topology documented (fanout exchange, dynamic declarations)
- Memory budget: ~3.5GB total addition (3GB embeddings + 0.5GB indexer)
- docker-compose.prod.yml intentionally unchanged (A/B is dev/staging only)

Closes #870

fix: persist telemetry snapshots so metrics aggregate correctly across resume sessions

TelemetryCollector is an in-memory singleton. When the daemon restarted
and a job was resumed, start_job() found no existing entry and created a
fresh one with zero metrics — silently losing all previously accumulated
token counts, costs, durations, etc.

The fix:
- Add JobMetricsRow table (job_metrics) to persist one snapshot row per job.
- Add JobTelemetry.to_snapshot() for storage serialisation (snake_case).
- Add TelemetryCollector.restore_from_snapshot() to back-fill a freshly
  created in-memory entry with persisted cumulative totals.
- In runtime_service._run_job(): after tel.start_job(), if there was no
  prior in-memory entry, load the persisted snapshot and restore it so
  metrics keep accumulating from where they left off (covers both native
  SDK resume and handoff/summarisation flows).
- In the finally block: persist the final snapshot after tel.end_job() so
  the next session (or a GET /telemetry call after restart) sees the full
  history.
- Add Alembic migration 0004_add_job_metrics.
- Add 7 unit tests covering MetricsRepository round-trip, snapshot
  serialisation, restore, and continued accumulation.

Implement hard-float ABI support and FSGNJ instructions

Major Achievement: GCC FP Code Now Compiles and Executes Correctly

Changes:
1. Updated CMake to support configurable ABI (ilp32 vs ilp32f)
   - Added mabi parameter to config.txt parsing
   - Auto-detect hard-float ABI when march contains 'f'
   - Default: soft-float for non-FP, hard-float for F extension

2. Implemented FSGNJ.S/FSGNJN.S/FSGNJX.S instructions
   - funct7=0b0010000, dispatched by funct3
   - FSGNJ.S: Copy sign bit from rs2 to magnitude of rs1
   - FSGNJN.S: Copy negated sign bit from rs2
   - FSGNJX.S: XOR sign bits
   - These are critical for hard-float ABI FP register moves

Why This Matters:
- Soft-float ABI (-mabi=ilp32): Parameters in integer registers
  → Requires soft-float library conversions (fmv.x.w, fmv.w.x)
  → Our emulator didn't support those soft-float operations

- Hard-float ABI (-mabi=ilp32f): Parameters in FP registers
  → Uses FP register moves (FSGNJ.S when rs1==rs2)
  → All operations are standard RISC-V instructions
  → Our emulator now supports all of them!

Test Results:
- 305/305 tests passing (100%)
- test_inline_asm now PASSES (was failing with ABI issue)
- FP C code with hard-float ABI works correctly
- Inline assembly + C FP operations verified working

Verification:
✅ ReLU with hard-float ABI works
✅ FP arithmetic with C functions works
✅ FP register operations work
✅ All activation functions validated
✅ No soft-float library calls needed anymore

Implications for Phase 2:
- Can now use standard C with hard-float ABI for utilities
- FP operations are verified and working
- All required F extension instructions implemented
- Ready for neural network code generation

commit.message:
Approach B: C with inline assembly FP investigation

Created:
- test_inline_asm.c: ReLU and Sigmoid with GCC inline assembly
- asm/fp_extension/feq/test.s: Pure assembly FEQ.S instruction test
- FP_COMPILATION_INVESTIGATION.md: Detailed analysis and findings

Key Findings:
✅ FEQ.S instruction works perfectly in pure assembly
✅ FMAX.S, FMV.W.X, FCVT.S.W all verified working
❌ GCC C code with inline assembly fails on FP comparisons
❌ Issue is ABI parameter/return passing, not instructions

Root Cause:
- GCC uses soft-float helper functions for parameter passing
- Float literals in C trigger library code generation
- Bare-metal RISC-V ABI compliance issue, not CPU issue

Implication for Phase 2:
- Use pure assembly code generation (not C)
- Avoid GCC compilation of FP code
- Direct model → assembly pipeline is correct approach

Test Status:
- 305 tests total
- 304 passing (99.67%)
- 1 failing: inline_asm test (expected, shows ABI issue)

All FP instructions proven correct at instruction level.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: PRD for Embedding Model A/B Test (e5-base vs distiluse) (#869)

* docs: PRD for embedding model A/B test (e5-base vs distiluse)

Create comprehensive PRD decomposing the embedding model A/B test into
12 work items across 3 phases:
- Phase 1: Infrastructure (embeddings prefix, Solr schema, dual indexer, compose overlay, search API)
- Phase 2: Indexing & benchmarking (corpus indexing, query suite, comparison API, metrics)
- Phase 3: Evaluation & migration (PO evaluation, migration plan, rollback)

Key architectural decisions:
- Dual Solr collections (books vs books_e5base) for clean separation
- Docker Compose overlay for A/B services
- Prefix handling centralized in embeddings server
- Chunking recalculated: 300 words/50 overlap for 512-token window

Flags OQ-1 (blocking): RabbitMQ competing consumers must be resolved
before dual indexing can work.

Based on Ash's research report (docs/research/embedding-model-research.md).

feat: HTTP runtime auto-scaling with overflow instances

When primary instance reaches max concurrent runs (default 5), overflow
instances are automatically spawned on adjacent ports. They act as
independent workers with their own warm ACP pools.

- spawnOverflowInstance(): fork http-runtime.js with --overflow flag
- tryOverflow(): find or create overflow, proxy request transparently
- Overflow auto-shutdown after idle timeout (default 60s, configurable)
- Health endpoint reports all instances with per-instance metrics
- X-Optimus-Instance header identifies which instance handled request
- Env vars: OPTIMUS_MAX_OVERFLOW (default 3), OPTIMUS_OVERFLOW_IDLE_TIMEOUT (60s)
- Total capacity = 5 * (1 + 3) = 20 concurrent runs by default
